articles.title: Emerging Market Stocks Fall the Most Since March as Oil Jumps
articles.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies declined after two consecutive sessions of gains, pressured by rising oil prices and higher bond yields.

articles.title: India’s Gold Demand Slows to Trickle on Tighter Trade Rules
articles.summary: India’s gold imports are slowing to a trickle as banks and bullion traders grapple with new restrictions aimed at shoring up a weak rupee battered by the Middle East war.

articles.title: BofA’s Hartnett Says Stock Market Ripe for Profit Taking in June
articles.summary: The stock market is ripe for profit-taking in early June due to investors crowding into equities and rising inflation risks, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

articles.title: Mexico’s Uber Crackdown at Airports to Complicate Plans for World Cup Visitors
articles.summary: Mexico is cracking down on the use of ride-hailing apps like Uber and Didi at the nation’s airports, causing headaches for travelers as it prepares to host the World Cup next month.

articles.title: Oil Heads for Weekly Gain With Iran War Negotiations at Impasse
articles.summary: Oil headed for a weekly gain as the crucial Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, with efforts to end the war in limbo and disruptions that have upended global markets set to linger.

articles.title: Stocks Hit as Inflation Angst Sends Yields Higher: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: A broad selloff in bond markets dragged stocks lower, putting a sudden halt to the artificial intelligence-fueled equity rally that has pushed the S&P 500 from one record high to the next.
